acceptance: a finite 100-to-300 case target is distributed across all required difficult-image categories
every future fixture is bound to immutable source and consent SHA-256 digests without publishing raw private assets
only reviewed ownership, consent or public-domain license classes are accepted
privacy review, bounded dimensions, bounded file size and deterministic corpus splits are mandatory
unknown provenance, missing consent, duplicate identity, private-data leakage or corpus shrinkage fails closed
phases.title: Real-world corpus intake foundation

acceptance: exactly 24 reviewed real-world assets are registered in the immutable qualification split
all ten difficult-image categories have at least one qualified case
source, consent and inspection evidence is bound to independent SHA-256 digests
source retrieval, consent, object immutability, decoding and privacy checks all pass
raw private assets and public PII never enter the repository
missing evidence, duplicate identity, mutable storage, privacy gaps or corpus shrinkage fails closed
phases.title: Qualified real-world corpus

acceptance: the exact qualified 24-case corpus is measured through the production pipeline three times per case
all 72 repeat samples are complete and bound to the immutable case-set and measurement head SHA
only one retry is allowed for bounded transient timeout or isolated-worker failures and every retry is audited
successful repeats for a case produce an identical SVG artifact SHA-256
a reviewed finite quality decision is emitted without fabricating missing metrics or claiming universal fidelity
RFV-4 remains blocked when the reviewed real-world release decision is no-go
phases.blocked_on: merge reviewed RFV-3 closure; release remains blocked on engine quality remediation and a passing real-world rerun
phases.title: Real pipeline measurement and automatic retry gate

phases.blocked_on: engine quality remediation and a passing real-world quality rerun with release_decision go
phases.title: Live beta validation and release evidence
